LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST ALL MOVING DEFENDANTS. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS COSTELLO III AND COSTELLO JR. AS THE FAC MEETS THE FACT PLEADING STANDARD REQUIRED BY THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 COURT DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTEENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ALL MOVING DEFENDANTS. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALLEN J. KENT, Tel No. (415) 272-2662 appearing for Plaintiff Christina A. Blake;
Atty. SHAUNA N. CORREIA of Weintraub, Tobin, Chediak, Coleman, Grodin, LC, Tel No. (916) 558-6000 appearing for Defendants Hill & Co. Property Management, Inc., Pamela Young, Robert Gebron, Joseph V. Costello, III, and Joseph Costello, Jr.

RULING - Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PAMELA YOUNG, ROBERT GEBRON, JOSEPH V. COSTELLO, III, AND JOSEPH COSTELLO, JR.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh and eighth causes of action against all moving Defendants. No opposition was raised and for good cause shown. The Demurrer is overruled as to the alter ego allegations against Defendants Costello III and Costello Jr. as the FAC meets the fact pleading standard required by the Code of Civil Procedure. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 court days leave to amend as to the sixteenth cause of action for all moving Defendants. First, the discovery rule is inapplicable here because Plaintiff necessarily knew of the facts that rendered the alleged representation untrue throughout the course of her employment, and when Plaintiff discovered the legal ramifications of those facts is irrelevant. (CCP 338(d).) However, the FAC also alleged that the misrepresentations continued throughout her employment. But, because this is a cause of action for fraud, the misrepresentations must be pled with sufficient particularity, and Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege with particularity any misrepresentations that fall within the statute of limitations. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALLEN J. KENT, Tel No. (415) 272-2662 appearing for Plaintiff Christina A. Blake;
Atty. SHAUNA N. CORREIA of Weintraub, Tobin, Chediak, Coleman, Grodin, LC, Tel No. (916) 558-6000 appearing for Defendants Hill & Co. Property Management, Inc., Pamela Young, Robert Gebron, Joseph V. Costello, III, and Joseph Costello, Jr.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HILL & CO. PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer overruled as to the ninth through fifteenth causes of action. A required element of collateral estoppel is that the party against whom it is asserted was a party to the prior proceeding or in privity with a party to the prior proceeding. (Zevnik v. Superior Court (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 76, 82, 70 Cal.Rptr.3d 817.) Plaintiff clearly was not a party to the Labor Commission action. Further, Defendant's contention that Plaintiff was in privity with Palmer in that action is based on an incorrect application of the "virtual representative" concept articulated in Alvarez v. May Dept. Stores Co.(2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1223. In that case, the plaintiffs in the prior proceeding sought and intended to be class representatives, and the court found that plaintiffs in a subsequent action, who were not parties to the initial action, were barred from asserting the same class claims. Notably, the court stated that collateral estoppel did not lead to an unfair result because the subsequent plaintiffs remained "free to litigate the merits of their personal claims. (Id. at 1238.) Thus, where class certification was denied, non-party plaintiffs were precluded from bringing another class action, but were not precluded from pursing their individual claims. The procedural posture here is distinguishable because the prior proceeding was not a class action, and Palmer did not act as or intend to represent a class. As such, the theory of "virtual representative" is inapplicable to these facts because it cannot be said that this plaintiff has had a full and fair opportunity to litigate the issue. Moreover, the FAC is not irreconcilable, as Defendant contends, because it is not so specific as to the class allegations that the determination of the purported class can necessarily be said to include Palmer. The Demurrer is overruled as to the tenth and thirteenth causes of action because Plaintiff's correction does not amount to a material inconsistency, but rather a correction to a typographical error. The demurrer is sustained with 10 court days leave to amend as to the sixteenth cause of action. First, the discovery rule is inapplicable here because Plaintiff necessarily knew of the facts that rendered the alleged representation untrue throughout the course of her employment, and when Plaintiff discovered the legal ramifications of those facts is irrelevant. (CCP 338(d).) However, the FAC also alleged that the misrepresentations continued throughout her employment. But, because this is a cause of action for fraud, the misrepresentations must be pled with sufficient particularity, and Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege with particularity any misrepresentations that fall within the statute of limitations. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
